jokes.text: Those girls are not bimbos, they're all career women.
jokes.setup: Jeff calls the calendar 'girlie' implying the women are objectified
jokes.punchline: Louie defends their honor as 'career women' while having a pin-up calendar
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - defending something ridiculous with mock seriousness. Good character voice.

jokes.text: That girl bending over to lace her boots is a brain surgeon.
jokes.setup: Louie claims the calendar women are career professionals
jokes.punchline: Describes an obviously sexualized pose as belonging to a brain surgeon
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the career women defense with specific absurd detail. Great economy.

jokes.text: that picture of your old lady give me the willies
jokes.setup: Jeff asks why his mother's picture is covered
jokes.punchline: Louie bluntly admits the picture scares him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie rudeness - no filter, perfect character integration.

jokes.text: you must admit she's no Lena Horne
jokes.setup: Defending his reaction to Jeff's mother's appearance
jokes.punchline: Compares her unfavorably to a famous beauty in understated way
jokes.explanation: Nice understatement technique, though the reference may be dated.

jokes.text: 'Cause God gave her beauty on the inside.
jokes.setup: Jeff defends his mother as beautiful
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's inner beauty, setting up Louie's response
jokes.explanation: Classic setup line - not funny itself but enables the punchline.

jokes.text: When I see that picture, I see the most beautiful woman in the world.
jokes.setup: Jeff threatens to deck Louie if he insults his mother again
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately caves and lies outrageously
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's cowardice, though predictable.

jokes.text: Sprinkle a little baby powder in your shorts.
jokes.setup: Alex mentions needing practice on his footwork
jokes.punchline: Jim misunderstands and offers medical advice for a different problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim misunderstanding - perfect character voice and misdirection.

jokes.text: Well, that's good for it, too, but if you really want to clear it up, just...
jokes.setup: Alex clarifies he was refereeing at a gym
jokes.punchline: Jim doubles down that baby powder still helps, whatever the problem is
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the misunderstanding - Jim's confidence in his remedy.

jokes.text: Every boy should know how to hop on a moving freight train.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jason's oboe lessons
jokes.punchline: Jim mishears 'oboe' as 'hobo' and offers life advice accordingly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim moment - the mishearing leads to perfectly delivered absurd advice. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Jim, I think she said oboe lessons, not hobo lessons.
jokes.setup: Jim's freight train advice
jokes.punchline: Tony clarifies the misunderstanding in deadpan fashion
jokes.explanation: Good straight man response to complete the bit, though not hilarious on its own.

jokes.text: For every hour that I practiced, I committed an atrocity.
jokes.setup: Louie claims he played violin without being called a sissy
jokes.punchline: His method was to balance music with violence
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Louie line - dark humor, perfect economy, totally in character. The word 'atrocity' elevates it.

jokes.text: Ballet dancing.
jokes.setup: Elaine lists activities Jason might prefer to oboe
jokes.punchline: Louie sarcastically suggests the most 'sissy' alternative
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing interruption, very economical, though the premise is predictable.

jokes.text: The neighborhood has changed.
jokes.setup: Jason says kids tease him for not playing beautifully enough
jokes.punchline: Louie deadpans that musical standards have risen in the neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Subtle and clever - treats musical criticism as sociological change. Good deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Yeah, you and the composer.
jokes.setup: Elaine says the oboe music is so beautiful it could make her cry
jokes.punchline: Louie suggests it would make the composer cry too (in a bad way)
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical putdown. Implies the music is so bad it would horrify its creator.

jokes.text: Hey, do they get beat up as bad as you used to?
jokes.setup: Tony explains he coaches kids now instead of boxing himself
jokes.punchline: Jason innocently references Tony's history of getting beaten up
jokes.explanation: Kid's innocent cruelty - references Tony's boxing failures without malice.

jokes.text: I think she has her heart set on logging or smelting pig iron.
jokes.setup: Jason asks why Elaine won't let him box
jokes.punchline: Louie references her earlier list of 'manly' alternatives to oboe
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier dialogue, shows Louie was listening and mocking.

jokes.text: That scene in the bar.
jokes.setup: Asked about his favorite part of Star Wars
jokes.punchline: Kid picks the cantina scene with weird aliens
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - kid likes the weird/violent parts, sets up the fake tooth gag.

jokes.text: Here, show this to your friends. They'll love it.
jokes.setup: Jason likes weird alien creatures
jokes.punchline: Louie gives him something (presumably false teeth) to show friends
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup - Louie corrupting a child with his dental prosthetics.

jokes.text: That's when his trunks usually start falling down.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if Tony lets kids fight until they hurt each other
jokes.punchline: Tony reveals the real reason fights end - wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up safety concern, delivers embarrassing physical reality.

jokes.text: Gregory is a wimp.
jokes.setup: Tony says Gregory is perfect for Jason to spar with
jokes.punchline: Bluntly states it's because Gregory is weak
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest assessment that makes everyone uncomfortable. Good economy.

jokes.text: 'The Butcher' Nardo.
jokes.setup: Gregory introduces himself as 'Bulldog'
jokes.punchline: Elaine gives her oboe-playing son an equally tough nickname
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - protective mom trying to sound tough. Ironic given Jason's nature.

jokes.text: I'm a blithering pea brain.
jokes.setup: Tony says she's not overprotective
jokes.punchline: Elaine realizes she's made a terrible mistake letting Jason box
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating line showing Elaine's immediate regret. 'Blithering pea brain' is colorful.

jokes.text: I've only boxed for ten seconds. I don't need a shower.
jokes.setup: Jason is told to hit the showers after winning quickly
jokes.punchline: Logically points out the short duration of his 'workout'
jokes.explanation: Kid logic applied to boxing clichés. Good character voice and timing.

jokes.text: You needed a shower when you got here.
jokes.setup: Jason says he doesn't need a shower after ten seconds of boxing
jokes.punchline: Tony implies Jason already had hygiene issues before boxing
jokes.explanation: Quick comeback to kid's logic. Economical and effective.

jokes.text: You said the same thing about Pac-Man.
jokes.setup: Elaine calls boxing appalling, disgusting, and dangerous
jokes.punchline: Jason reveals she used the same arguments against video games
jokes.explanation: Brilliant kid comeback that reveals parental hypocrisy. Highly quotable for its relatability.

jokes.text: The ability of two men to put on gloves, stand toe-to-toe in the spirit of sportsmanship and pummel each other into insensibility is what separates us from the animals.
jokes.setup: Jim offers 'pertinent words' about boxing
jokes.punchline: Delivers a mock-philosophical defense that contradicts itself
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Jim moment - pseudo-intellectual rambling that's accidentally profound and absurd. The contradiction between 'sportsmanship' and 'insensibility' is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: you wouldn't be hearing me now unless you were a dog.
jokes.setup: Alex says where ten-year-olds hit him, they can't do much damage
jokes.punchline: Implies they hit him so low only dogs could hear his voice
jokes.explanation: Clever way to reference getting hit in the groin without saying it directly. Creative metaphor.

jokes.text: Well, no, actually, we were playing soccer at the time. We... not allowed to use your hands in soccer, so it cost us a penalty kick.
jokes.setup: Alex describes his triumphant moment catching a ball and running
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was the wrong sport entirely, making his 'triumph' a disaster
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds up a sports triumph then reveals it was actually a major error.

jokes.text: And, uh, well, after the game, the kids on the team took off my pants and threw them out of the bus window, but that didn't matter, no. I mean, I felt, I felt accepted.
jokes.setup: Alex's soccer mistake led to team punishment
jokes.punchline: Despite humiliation, he somehow felt it meant acceptance
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny character moment showing Alex's desperate need for acceptance even through humiliation.

jokes.text: I should have been a lawyer.
jokes.setup: Tony gives a weak argument for Jason boxing
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically assesses his persuasive abilities
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating comment about his poor argumentative skills. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: No, I do.
jokes.setup: Asked if his son hangs around pool halls
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's the one hanging around pool halls
jokes.explanation: Quick misdirection revealing the father's own questionable habits. Very economical.

jokes.text: Your son hangs around pool halls? / No, I do.
jokes.setup: Discussion of why boxing beats hanging around pool halls
jokes.punchline: The father's admission of his own poor habits rather than his son's
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and self-aware parenting humor. Clean setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: I blame him and I blame him!
jokes.setup: Father says he couldn't blame his son for anything in competition
jokes.punchline: Elaine dramatically assigns blame to both boys
jokes.explanation: Emotional mother overreaction. Shows character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Look how good it healed.
jokes.setup: Tony says not to worry about Jason's broken nose because his was broken six times
jokes.punchline: Points to his obviously crooked/damaged nose as evidence
jokes.explanation: Visual gag requiring Tony's messed-up nose. Good ironic 'reassurance' that makes things worse.

jokes.text: Not a pretty picture: a 44-year-old man sitting in a room full of hair, playing an oboe.
jokes.setup: Elaine suggests Jason could grow a beard instead of shaving
jokes.punchline: Alex paints a vivid picture of the future consequences
jokes.explanation: Vivid absurd imagery combining overprotective parenting with musical dedication. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You gotta be kidding.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests Jason might want to box again after his traumatic experience
jokes.punchline: Jason's incredulous response reveals Alex was completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good button ending - subverts Alex's assumption and shows lesson learned. Economical.
